- So, hei ass mäi Programm fir de Mettesch.
- Ech fuere lo an d'Stad, da ginn ech direkt op de Biergercenter, meng nei Carte d'Identitéit sichen.
- Ech muss och nach an d'Apdikt, Sue op hiewen a bei de Coiffeur.
- Ech gi virdéischt op de Bankomat, dann hunn ech mol rëm Sue an der Täsch.
- Duerno ginn ech meng Medikamenter an d'Apdikt sichen an da ginn ech mir eng Zeitung kafen, fir mech beim Coiffeur net ze langweilen.
- Um véier Auer hunn ech do RDV.
- Ech mengen, fir an d'Bibliothéik bleift keng Zäit méi.
- Dat ass net schlëmm, da maachen ech dat nächst Kéier.
- So, this is my program for the Mettesch.
- I drive into the city, then I go straight to the citizen center to search for my new identity card.
- I also have to go to the pharmacy, lift money, and go to the hairdresser.
- I first go to the ATM, then I have cash in my pocket again.
- After that I go get my medications at the pharmacy and buy myself a newspaper to avoid getting bored at the hairdresser.
- At four o'clock I have an appointment there.
- I don't think there's time to stay in the library.
- That's not bad, I'll do that next time.

- Jo.

- Here is a card and here are the books.
- Thank you, until next time.
- Adi.

- Wisst dir schon, wat dir huelt?
- Jo, ech huelen Zalot vum Dach.
- Et dët malet, et ass keng Mido.
- Dann hätt ech gärt Geméiszop als Entri an zënner de Begel mat Zoma.
- Ech huelen och Zop als Entri, an dann en italienische Sandwich.
- Hooni Ham, as di vegetäria.
- Oh, an de Zop as awer Speck.
- An Hooni Speck, geht da doch?
- Nee, dat geht leider net.
- Dann huelen ischte Geméiszop.
- Do ass jo Soschakee Speck dran.
- Nee, nee.
- A fir ze drinken?
- Brengt mir en halwe Liter Sprudelwasser.
- Ech hätt gäär e Glas Rose, wannisch knift.
- Et dët malet, me mir verkäfe keen Alkohol.
- Dann huelen ischoch Wasser.
- Anderrei, also zweemol en halwe Liter Sprudel.
- Jo, wannisch knift.
- Et das jo ganz flott hei, me Musik as ze haart.
- Oh, sou haart asse dach net.
- Sou, haio set e bësse käl.
- Jo, 15 Hohannen as ob.
- Madame, könnt die 15 Zoumechen, wannisch knift.
- Jo, kee Problem.
- Im Moment, hei sind ja Entrien.
- Mmh, dat riecht aber gut.
- Jo, du wasch escht.
- Guten Appetit.
- Gute Appetit.
- Do you already know what you want to drink?
- Yes, I’ll take Zalot from the roof.
- It dyes it, it isn’t mid.
- Then I would like Geméiszop as an entry and a can of Begel with Zoma.
- I’ll also take Zop as an entry, then an Italian sandwich.
- Hooni Ham, are you vegetarian?
- Oh, and the Zop is still bacon.
- And Hooni bacon, does that work?
- No, that doesn’t work unfortunately.
- Then I’ll take Geméiszop.
- There is Soschakee bacon on it.
- No, no.
- And to drink?
- Bring me a half liter of sparkling water.
- I’d like a glass of rosé, if you have it.
- It dyes it, but we don’t sell any alcohol.
- Then we’ll take water.
- Another one, also two times a half liter of sparkling.
- Yes, if you have it.
- It goes very quickly here, but the music is too loud.
- Oh, it’s not that loud here.
- So, turn it down a bit.
- Yes, 15 o’clock is up.
- Madam, could you give me the 15 Zoumechen if you have it.
- Yes, no problem.
- At the moment, there are Entrien.
- Mmh, that smells good.
- Yes, you wash it well.
- Enjoy your meal.
- Enjoy your meal.

- Listen now to the dialogue.

- Salut Juliette, wéi geet et?
- Ah, Moin Jasmin, mir geet et tipptopp.
- An dir?
- Och gutt, merci.
- Wat maachs du dann hei an der Stad?
- Maja, ech ginn nëmmen hei un d'Abtekt, an um zwee Auer hunn ech en Rendezvous bei der Ästheticienne.
- Oh, wou ass dann hei eng Ästheticienne?
- Direkt hei bei der Abtekt.
- An du, wat maachs du haut?
- Ech ginn Kado kafen.
- Mäi Mann an meng Schwëster hunn nächst Woch Gebuertsdag.
- Wousst du schonn deng Iddi?
- Jo, ech ginn nëmmen fir dech an d'Bischofboutique e Buch iwwer Fotografie fir de Laurent kafen, an dann ginn ech an d'Parfümerie.
- Do fannen ech bestëmmt eppes fir meng Schwëster.
- Also, ech ginn dann e Mond op eng Hochzäit, an ech brauch nach eppes Schickes.
- Kenns du eng flott Kleederboutique?
- Jo, sécher.
- Ech weess den Numm net méi, mee ech erklären dir de Wee.
- Du gees hei richteg aus, léiers Bank, an dann bei der Kräizung nëmmen riets.
- Ah, do, wou d'Bibliothéik ass.
- Jo, genee, an dann hälts du déi zweet Strooss lénks, d'Boutique also wäit.
- Ah, flott, merci.
- Ech ginn dohinner an kucken.
- Ech hu eng Iddi.
- Ech ginn nëmmen mäi Kado kafen, dann ginn ech no Seropost, un d'Bank, an dann kommen ech och un d'Kleederboutique.
- Zu zwee ass dat méi flott.
- Super, an dono ginn mir op Plus eppes drénken.
- Tipptopp, do bass du jo nach.
- Jo, bis dann.
- Hello Juliette, how are you?
- Ah, good morning Jasmin, I’m doing tip-top.
- And you?
- Also good, thanks.
- What are you doing here in town then?
- Maja, I’m only here at the department store, and at two o’clock I have a date with the beautician.
- Oh, where is a beautician here then?
- Right here at the department store.
- And you, what are you doing today?
- I’m going to buy a gift.
- My husband and my sister have a birthday next week.
- Do you already know your idea?
- Yes, I’m only buying for you in the Bishop Boutique a book about photography for Laurent and then I’m going to the perfume shop.
- There I’ll surely find something for my sister.
- So, I’ll then buy a ring for a wedding, and I still need something chic.
- Do you know a nice clothing boutique?
- Yes, certainly.
- I don’t know the name anymore, but I’ll explain the way to you.
- You go straight out here, next to the bank, and then at the intersection turn right.
- Ah, there, where the library is.
- Yes, exactly, and then you take the second street on the left, the boutique is quite far.
- Ah, nice, thanks.
- I’ll go there now and have a look.
- I have an idea.
- I’m only going to buy my gift, then I’ll go to Seropost, by the bank, and then I’ll also come to the clothing boutique.
- Two of us is nicer.
- Great, and afterwards we’ll have a drink at Plus.
- Tipptopp, you’re still there then.
- Yes, see you then.

- Den Depart vun Tour ass bei der Gëlle Fra.
- D'Gëlle Fra ass e Monument fir d'Lëtzebuerger Soldaten, déi am Éischte Weltkrich gestuerwe sinn.
- Si ass 21 Meter héich a steet op der Konstitutiounsplaz.
- Vun do hutt Dir eng schéi Vue op de Péitrussdall.
- The departure of Tour is at the Golden Girl.
- The Golden Lady is a monument for the Luxembourgish soldiers who died in the First World War.
- It is 21 meters tall and stands on Constitution Square.
- From there you have a beautiful view of the Petrusse Valley.

- Direkt bei der Gëlle Fra ass d'Entrée vun de Kasematten.
- Dat sinn Tunnellen ënnert der Stad Lëtzebuerg, déi zesummen ongeféier zwanzeg Kilometer laang sinn.
- Kasematte sinn am Fréijoer an am Summer fir Visite op.
- Dat ass interessant, an dat géife mer elo kucken.
- Directly at the Gëlle Fra is the entrance to the Kasematten.
- Those are tunnels under the city of Luxembourg, which together are about twenty kilometers long.
- Kasematten are open for visits in spring and summer.
- That is interesting, and we would look at that now.

- Mir ginn op d'Plaz eppes drénken.
- Den offiziellen Numm vun der Plaz ass Plaz d'Armes.
- Do si vill Caféen a Restauranten mat Terrassen.
- Et sinn ëmmer vill Leit do.
- Um Kiosk an der Mëtt vun der Plaz sinn am Summer vill Concerten.
- After visiting the Casemates, we take a small break.
- We are going to the square to drink something.
- The official name of the square is Place d'Armes.
- There are many cafes and restaurants with terraces.
- There are always many people there.
- At the kiosk in the middle of the square there are many concerts in the summer.

- Do ass den Touristbüro, d'Gemeng an eng Statu vum Wëllem II op engem Päerd.
- Den offizielle Numm vun der Plaz ass Place Guillaume II.
- Hei ass mëttwochs a samschdes vu siwe bis zwou Auer Maart.
- From the square, we go to the Knuedler.
- Here is the tourist office, the commune, and a statue of William II on a horse.
- The official name of the square is Place Guillaume II.
- Here is Wednesday and Saturday from seven to two o'clock market.

- Vum Knuedler brauche mer just eng Minutt bis bei de Palais.
- Dat ass dem Grand-Duc seng Residenz an der Stad.
- D'Architektur ass am Renaissance-Stil an am Barock-Stil.
- Mir hu kee Rendez-vous fir eng Visite, dofir kucke mir de Palais just vu baussen.
- Riets vum Palais ass d'Chamber, dat Lëtzebuerger Parlament.
- From the Knuedler we only need a minute to get to the Palace.
- It is the Grand Duke's residence and the city.
- The architecture is in Renaissance style and Baroque style.
- We have no appointment for a tour, so we will just view the palace from the outside.
- The palace’s direction is the Chamber, the Luxembourg Parliament.

- Mir sinn hei an der Fousgängerzoun.
- Mir gesinn vill Boutiquen.
- E Kleedergeschäft, e Schonggeschäft, eng Zeitungsboutik, eng Parfümerie an eng Optik.
- Nieft der Optik ass eng Ästheticien.
- Op der Terrasse vun der Bäckerei sëtze vill Leit an der Sonn an drénken eppes.
- E Musiker spillt Saxofon an e puer Leit lauschteren nu.
- Et gëtt eng Post an e Bankomat.
- Hanne gesi mir méi Kär.
- We are here in the pedestrian zone.
- We see many boutiques.
- A clothing store, a shoe store, a newspaper shop, a perfume shop, and an optical shop.
- Next to the optical shop is an esthetician.
- On the terrace of the bakery, many people sit in the sun and drink something.
- A musician plays the saxophone and a few people listen now.
- There is a post office and an ATM.
- Hanne, see we more core.

- Bonjour, ech hätt gären e Billier fir op Frankfurt.
- Wéi ni wälte fueren?
- Mäde moien.
- Fir dir ge erst Klass oder zweit Klass?
- Zweit Klass.
- E Moment, ech gucken.
- Et deed mir leed, zweit Klass as voll.
- Ante isch Klass?
- Hastu noch Platz?
- Jo, du sind noch e pur Plätze frei.
- Also dein größt Differenz am Preis?
- Teisch Klass as fünfundzwanzeg Euro me da ja.
- De Billier kascht achzig Euro.
- Und wie viel Auer fährt den Zug?
- Um Härdau a ferian zwanzeg, an um zwiele Wauer ferian zwanzeg sind dir zu Frankfurt.
- Andrei, es shule in de Billier.
- Bezölle boa oder matta Kart?
- Matta Kart.
- Dann isch klift, hai as der e Billier.
- Edi an eng gud res.
- Merci, Edi.
- Hello, I would like a ticket to Frankfurt.
- What are you willing to pay?
- Good morning.
- For you first class or second class?
- Second class.
- One moment, I’m looking.
- I’m sorry, second class is full.
- What class is it?
- Do you have any seats left?
- Yes, there are still a few seats free.
- So your biggest difference in price?
- Second class is twenty-five euros more than yes.
- The ticket costs eighty euros.
- And how much time does the train take?
- To Härdau and back twenty, and to Zweilau back twenty, are you to Frankfurt.
- Andrei, it’s cheaper in the ticket.
- Pay here or with a card?
- With a card.
- Then it’s settled, here is your ticket.
- Edi and a good one.
- Thanks, Edi.

- Bonjour, ech hätt gär e Billjee fir op Frankfurt.
- Wéi niewel der fueren?
- Muer de Moien.
- Fuert der éischte Klass oder zweet Klass?
- Zweet Klass.
- E Moment, ech kucken.
- Et deet mir leed, zweet Klass ass voll.
- An éischte Klass?
- Hast du nach Platz?
- Jo, do sinn nach e puer Platze frei.
- Also den gréisste Virdeel am Präis?
- Éischte Klass ass 25 Euro méi deier, de Billjee kascht 80 Euro.
- A wéi villäer fiert den Zuch?
- Um Aacht Auer fäerten 20 an um Zwielef Auer fäerten 20 si dir zu Frankfurt.
- An da hei, ech schécken de Billjee.
- Bezuelst du bar oder mat der Kaart?
- Mat der Kaart.
- Dann ass et gutt, hei äre Billjee.
- Merci, an eng gutt Rees.
- Merci, Iech och.
- Hello, I’d like a ticket to Frankfurt.
- How many stops are you driving?
- Good morning.
- Are you driving first class or second class?
- Second class.
- One moment, I’m looking.
- I’m sorry, second class is full.
- And first class?
- Do you have any seats left?
- Yes, there are still a few seats available.
- So the biggest advantage in price?
- First class is 25 Euro more expensive, the ticket costs 80 Euro.
- And how much does the train fare?
- At eight o’clock depart 20 and at twelve o’clock depart 20 you’re in Frankfurt.
- And then here, I’ll send you the ticket.
- Do you pay cash or by card?
- By card.
- Then that’s fine, here is your ticket.
- Thanks, and have a good trip.
- Thank you, same to you.

- Entschuldigt, wuhu krene gebus für an den Zentrum?
- Dat ass ganz einfach, Monsieur.
- Dir geht erous an dann noch rechts.
- Bei der roter Lucht geht er iwer d'Stroos.
- Do sind Busaräen.
- Busse fuhre bald all an d'Stadt.
- Frot einfach de Buschauffeur.
- Gedit ke Plan vun de Busse?
- Doch, hei assen.
- Du geseht dir alt Buslinien an d'Stadt.
- Villmohts Merci für die Information.
- Das gern geschieht, Monsieur.
- Noch en schönen Dach.
- Merci gleichfalls.
- Awa.
- Excuse me, which bus goes to the center?
- That is quite simple, Monsieur.
- You go straight and then still right.
- At the red light you go over the street.
- There are buses.
- Buses drive soon all into the city.
- Just ask the bus driver.
- Do you have a plan of the buses?
- Yes, here is one.
- You see all bus lines in the city.
- Thank you very much for the information.
- You’re welcome, Monsieur.
- Have a nice day.
- Thank you likewise.
- Bye.

- an da sidd Dir schonn beim Palais.
- Villmools Merci.
- Dat ass gär geschitt.
- Sorry, please, how do I get to the Palace?

- and then you’re already at the Palace.
- Thank you very much.
- You’re welcome.

- Zwe, entschëllegt, Franneschglift, wei kommen ich bei de Mudam?
- Zu Fouss oder mam Bus?
- Zu Fouss, wehre aus Schéin an e Schogutzeit.
- Major, da gidd dir de Boulevard Royale rup, lanscht den Uertel an op der Kreizung no lenks.
- Dadas d'Avenue de la Porte Neuf.
- Dir gitt lanscht de Park an op der Kreizung no riets.
- Du nogidd dir iwer Trottbréck an dann erun gefäuer e Kilometer i Schdaus.
- Dir gesitt de Musée op der Riethsazäit.
- Merci!
- Gern gesitt!
- Sorry, excuse me, Franneschglift, how do I get to the Mudam?
- On foot or by bus?
- On foot, go through Schön and a short time.
- Sir, then you go onto Boulevard Royale, past the Uertel and at the intersection to the left.
- You go past the Park and at the intersection to the right.
- You go up Trottbréck and then slowly about a kilometer to Schdaus.
- You will see the Musée at the Riethsazäit.
- Thank you!
- You’re welcome!
